title: festuca indigesta subsp. alleizettei
intro: Festuca indigesta subsp. alleizettei, also known as Festuca alleizettei, is a subspecies of fescue, a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and its health benefits include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and relieving respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Festuca alleizettei include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
